pi_items.source: Task specification and ICAO airport database verification. KLAX is the official ICAO code for Los Angeles International Airport.
Task specification and ICAO airport database verification. KSAN is the official ICAO code for San Diego International Airport.
Task specification and FAA navigation aid database. SLI is the official identifier for Seal Beach VORTAC located in Los Alamitos, California.
Task description explicitly states the route sequence must be 'KLAX -> SLI -> KSAN'. Verified against task.json description field.

Web search on official airport coordinate databases (travelmath.com, AirNav KLAX). Los Angeles International Airport official coordinates verified from https://www.travelmath.com/airport/LAX and https://metar-taf.com/airport/KLAX-los-angeles-international-airport

Web search on FAA navigation aid database. Seal Beach VORTAC coordinates verified from http://www.airnav.com and https://opennav.com/navaid/US/SLI/SEAL+BEACH. Located in Los Alamitos, California. Metadata values are appropriately rounded representations of actual coordinates.

Web search on official airport coordinate databases. San Diego International Airport (Lindbergh Field) coordinates verified from https://www.travelmath.com/airport/SAN and https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/us/united-states/3143/san-diego-international-airport

Coordinate matching tolerance for the task verifier. One degree of latitude/longitude at sea level is approximately 111 kilometers, so 0.05 degrees = ~5.55 km tolerance, which is reasonable for aviation flight plan verification.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified as accurate. The task uses real airport and navigation aid identifiers: KLAX (Los Angeles International, 33.9425°N, 118.4080°W), KSAN (San Diego International, 32.7335°N, 117.1897°W), and SLI (Seal Beach VORTAC, 33.7833°N, 118.0548°W). The expected flight plan route sequence is KLAX → SLI → KSAN, operating on real-world aviation data. The 0.05-degree tolerance is appropriate for this coordinate matching verification. The data is not synthetic—it uses real ICAO airport codes and FAA-registered navigation aid identifiers with actual geographic coordinates.
